title: lysimachia kipahuluensis subsp. caliginis
intro: Lysimachia kipahuluensis subsp. caliginis is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Lysimachia genus, commonly known as the ma'o hau hele. It is a flowering plant native to Hawaii, characterized by its yellow flowers and red stems. The medicinal properties of Lysimachia kipahuluensis subsp. caliginis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use.
